channel.title: OnOptions
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from OnOptions, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Pope Leo says he carries a photo of a slain Muslim boy in his pocket: ‘I cannot be in favor of war’
item.description: Leo also suggested that the U.S. church is excessively focused on sex.

item.title: The older you get, the harder it is to make friends at work. Here’s why
item.description: After-work drinks are a nice way to bond with colleagues in your 20s and 30s. But, as people get older, different circumstances can necessitate more p...

item.title: Former U.S. Cyber Director Sounds the Alarm on Anthropic’s ‘Too Powerful’ AI Model
item.description: The company’s new Mythos system can autonomously find and exploit software vulnerabilities, but at what cost?

item.title: ‘Godmother of Silicon Valley’ Esther Wojcicki, mother of the YouTube and 23andMe CEOs, shares her secret to raising future leaders
item.description: Wojcicki takes inspiration from her daughters’ public failures and how they pivoted to turn mistakes into successful companies.

item.title: Tesla Just Placed a $2 Billion Bet on AI Hardware—With Almost No Details
item.description: The company quietly slipped the deal into a recent regulatory filing

item.title: Microsoft is making this first-ever move to cut workforce size amid the AI shift
item.description: Microsoft is offering voluntary buyouts to select employees, it announced in a memo on Thursday, CNBC reported . The move is a first for the company, ...

item.title: People living in the U.S. with temporary protected status, targeted by Trump, are a $29 billion economic force
item.description: As the Supreme Court prepares to weigh the fate of TPS for Haitians and Syrians, a new report accounts for a demographic’s economic impact.

item.title: Google CEO Sundar Pichai says 75% of the company’s code is AI-generated
item.description: From software engineers to non-technical staff, Google has urged its employees to fully embrace AI . And it seems like the push to use the tech has re...

item.title: Lowe’s faces pressure to cut ties with Flock Safety as AI surveillance data raises serious privacy concerns
item.description: Lowe’s Home Improvement is facing pressure to cut ties with Flock Safety, the surveillance company that makes cameras, drones, and automated license p...

item.title: Meta, Microsoft look to trim workforces amid heavy AI spending
item.description: Meta told personnel in an internal memo on Thursday that it planned to cut 10% of workers.

item.title: The $320 Million Freeze: A Billionaire Is Taking Trump’s Crypto Venture to Court, but the Company Just Fired Back
item.description: A crypto entrepreneur claims World Liberty Financial illegally froze $320 million worth of his tokens. The Trump family disagrees.

item.title: Duolingo Won the Internet With Chaos. Now Its CMO Says It’s Time to Rein It In
item.description: There’s a new green owl in town, and it’s going to be a little less unhinged.

item.title: ‘It was like this little gift that fell in my lap’: Millions of Americans may soon discover they’ve been Canadian all along
item.description: If you have a grandparent, great-grandparent or even more distant ancestor from Canada, you could be Canadian.

item.title: All Aboard the Freelance Revolution
item.description: How to attract and onboard independent, project-based talent to unlock business agility

item.title: One viral JetBlue blunder has customers convinced it uses surveillance pricing to upcharge on flights
item.description: JetBlue replies to customers on social media every day, from assuaging their customer service woes to thanking them for choosing their flights. But on...
